title: LHP Schema / Tags File
description: Unified schema/tags file (schemas/<table>.yaml). One file that a write action can point BOTH 'table_schema' (column types -> schema hints) and 'tags_file' (Unity Catalog table/column tags) at; each reader consumes only its own fields. The identifier is optional: 'table' is canonical, with 'name' accepted as an alias. The legacy schema keys 'version'/'description'/'primary_key' are tolerated and ignored.

table.description: Optional identifier (the table name). Canonical: wins over 'name' when both are present. When the file is used as a 'tags_file', it should equal the write target's table; a mismatch logs a warning (LHP-CFG-068) and generation proceeds using the write target's table.

name.description: Accepted alias for 'table' (schema-file / ODCS convention). 'table' is canonical and wins when both are present; a differing 'name' logs a warning (LHP-CFG-068).

tags.description: Table-level Unity Catalog tags. Consumed by the 'tags_file' reader; ignored by the 'table_schema' reader. An explicit empty mapping means 'managed with an empty set' under remove_undeclared_tags.

columns.description: Column entries. The 'table_schema' reader consumes name/type/nullable/comment; the 'tags_file' reader consumes name/tags.

type.description: SQL type (e.g. STRING, BIGINT). Required for 'table_schema' use; optional when the file is tags-only.

nullable.description: Whether the column is nullable (schema use). Defaults to true.

tags.description: This column's Unity Catalog tags (tags_file use; may be empty). Absent means the column contributes no tags.

version.description: Legacy schema key -- tolerated and ignored by both readers.

description.description: Legacy schema key -- tolerated and ignored by both readers.

primary_key.description: Legacy schema key -- tolerated and ignored by both readers.
